items.docs:
Returns a page of queues registered in the platform. You can specify parameters to filter the results. The result is wrapped with PageData object that allows you to iterate over result set using pagination. See response schema for more details. 

Available for users with 'SYS_ADMIN' or 'TENANT_ADMIN' authority.

params.name: serviceType
params.description: Service type (implemented only for the TB-RULE-ENGINE)

items.docs:
Create or update the Queue. When creating queue, platform generates Queue Id as [time-based UUID](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Universally_unique_identifier#Version_1_(date-time_and_MAC_address)). Specify existing Queue id to update the queue. Referencing non-existing Queue Id will cause 'Not Found' error.

Queue name is unique in the scope of sysadmin. Remove 'id', 'tenantId' from the request body example (below) to create new Queue entity. 

Available for users with 'SYS_ADMIN' authority.

params.name: queueId
params.description: A string value representing the queue id. For example, '784f394c-42b6-435a-983c-b7beff2784f9'

items.docs:
Fetch the Queue object based on the provided Queue Id. 

Available for users with 'SYS_ADMIN' or 'TENANT_ADMIN' authority.

params.name: queueId
params.description: A string value representing the queue id. For example, '784f394c-42b6-435a-983c-b7beff2784f9'

items.docs:
Deletes the Queue. 

Available for users with 'SYS_ADMIN' authority.

params.name: queueName
params.description: A string value representing the queue id. For example, 'Main'

items.docs:
Fetch the Queue object based on the provided Queue name. 

Available for users with 'SYS_ADMIN' or 'TENANT_ADMIN' authority.

items.docs: Returns a page of events for specified entity. Deprecated and will be removed in next minor release. The call was deprecated to improve the performance of the system. Current implementation will return 'Lifecycle' events only. Use 'Get events by type' or 'Get events by filter' instead. You can specify parameters to filter the results. The result is wrapped with PageData object that allows you to iterate over result set using pagination. See response schema for more details.

items.docs:
Returns a page of events for the chosen entity by specifying the event filter. You can specify parameters to filter the results. The result is wrapped with PageData object that allows you to iterate over result set using pagination. See response schema for more details. 

# Event Filter Definition

6 different eventFilter objects could be set for different event types. The eventType field is required. Others are optional. If some of them are set, the filtering will be applied according to them.

info.name: Creates or Updates the Administration Settings (saveAdminSettings)

items.docs:
Creates or Updates the Administration Settings. Platform generates random Administration Settings Id during settings creation. The Administration Settings Id will be present in the response. Specify the Administration Settings Id when you would like to update the Administration Settings. Referencing non-existing Administration Settings Id will cause an error.

Available for users with 'SYS_ADMIN' authority.

Attempts to send test sms to the System Administrator User using SMS Settings and phone number provided as a parameters of the request. 

Available for users with 'SYS_ADMIN' authority.

Attempts to send test email to the System Administrator User using Mail Settings provided as a parameter. You may change the 'To' email in the user profile of the System Administrator. 

Available for users with 'SYS_ADMIN' authority.

Get the Security Settings object that contains password policy, etc.

Available for users with 'SYS_ADMIN' authority.

Updates the Security Settings object that contains password policy, etc.

Available for users with 'SYS_ADMIN' authority.

Get the repository settings object. 

Available for users with 'TENANT_ADMIN' authority.

Available for users with 'SYS_ADMIN' authority.

Updates the JWT Settings object that contains JWT token policy, etc. The tokenSigningKey field is a Base64 encoded string.

Available for users with 'SYS_ADMIN' authority.

Check whether the repository settings exists. 

Available for users with 'TENANT_ADMIN' authority.

info.name: Get OAuth2 Log in Processing URL (getMailProcessingUrl)

items.docs:
Returns the URL enclosed in double quotes. After successful authentication with OAuth2 provider and user consent for requested scope, it makes a redirect to this path so that the platform can do further log in processing and generating access tokens. 

Available for users with 'SYS_ADMIN' authority.

info.name: Redirect User to Mail Provider Login Page.

items.docs: After user logged in and provided accessprovider sends authorization code to specified redirect uri.)

Get information about enabled/disabled features. 

Available for users with 'SYS_ADMIN' authority.

Check whether the auto commit settings exists. 

Available for users with 'TENANT_ADMIN' authority.

params.name: pageSize
params.description: Maximum amount of entities in a one page

params.name: page
params.description: Sequence number of page starting from 0

params.name: textSearch
params.description: The case insensitive 'substring' filter based on one of the next properties: entityType, entityName, userName, actionType, actionStatus.

params.name: sortProperty
params.description: Property of audit log to sort by. See the 'Model' tab of the Response Class for more details. Note: entityType sort property is not defined in the AuditLog class, however, it can be used to sort audit logs by types of entities that were logged.

params.name: sortOrder
params.description: Sort order. ASC (ASCENDING) or DESC (DESCENDING)

params.name: startTime
params.description: The start timestamp in milliseconds of the search time range over the AuditLog class field: 'createdTime'.

params.name: endTime
params.description: The end timestamp in milliseconds of the search time range over the AuditLog class field: 'createdTime'.

params.name: actionTypes
params.description: A String value representing comma-separated list of action types. This parameter is optional, but it can be used to filter results to fetch only audit logs of specific action types. For example, 'LOGIN', 'LOGOUT'. See the 'Model' tab of the Response Class for more details.

items.docs:
Returns a page of audit logs related to all entities in the scope of the current user's Tenant. You can specify parameters to filter the results. The result is wrapped with PageData object that allows you to iterate over result set using pagination. See response schema for more details. 

Available for users with 'TENANT_ADMIN' authority.

items.docs:
Returns a page of audit logs related to the actions of targeted user. For example, RPC call to a particular device, or alarm acknowledgment for a specific device, etc. You can specify parameters to filter the results. The result is wrapped with PageData object that allows you to iterate over result set using pagination. See response schema for more details. 

Available for users with 'TENANT_ADMIN' authority.

items.docs:
Returns a page of audit logs related to the actions on the targeted entity. Basically, this API call is used to get the full lifecycle of some specific entity. For example to see when a device was created, updated, assigned to some customer, or even deleted from the system. You can specify parameters to filter the results. The result is wrapped with PageData object that allows you to iterate over result set using pagination. See response schema for more details. 

Available for users with 'TENAN

items.docs:
Returns a page of audit logs related to the targeted customer entities (devices, assets, etc.), and users actions (login, logout, etc.) that belong to this customer. You can specify parameters to filter the results. The result is wrapped with PageData object that allows you to iterate over result set using pagination. See response schema for more details. 

Available for users with 'TENANT_ADMIN' authority.
